---
title: "Practice with CSVs, Section 1"
format: html
---

# Making a DataFrame from a `dict`

This is **bold** text, this is *empasized text*,

Put some notes here

```{python}
import pandas as pd

```

# Making a DataFrame from a `dict`

This is **bold** text, this is *empasized text*,

Put some notes here

```{python}
favs = {"show" : ["Breaking Bad", "Prison Break", "The Office", "Andor"],
        "Likes" : [13, 5, 10, 1],
        "Dislikes" : [1, 0, 3, 0]}
favs_df = pd.DataFrame(favs)

favs_df
```

# Simple CSV reading

```{python}
random_data = pd.read_csv("2-11-26/random_correlated_data.csv")

random_data


```

# Messy CSV reading

## When theres junk at the top

- Link to pandas doc on read_CSV

https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/reference/api/pandas.read_csv.html

```{python}
pymovies = pd.read_csv("2-11-26/python_movies.csv")
pymovies
pymovies.dtypes

pymovies = pd.read_csv("2-11-26/python_movies.csv", header = 5)
pymovies
pymovies.dtypes

```


## Here's the problem 


```{python}
import pandas as pd

df = pd.read_csv("2-11-26/python_movies_mod3.csv", skiprows=5)
print(df.head())

```
